name: gobuster (Directory/DNS/S3 Brute-Forcer)

auth.notes: Pass username/password or cookies for authenticated scans

performance.notes: Go-based concurrent scanner; -t flag controls thread count

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-mode brute forcer. Modes: dir (directories), dns (subdomains), vhost (virtual hosts), s3 (S3 buckets), fuzz (generic fuzzing), gcs (Google Cloud Storage). gobuster dir -u https://target -w wordlist.txt. gobuster dns -d target.com -w subdomains.txt. -t threads. -o output.txt. --status-codes-blacklist 404. -x file extensions. No recursive (use feroxbuster). CTF staple alongside nmap.

gotchas: DNS mode requires DNS resolver access
S3 mode has false positives on misconfigured error pages
avoid_when: JSON output requirements (ffuf handles this better)
summary: gobuster is the classic Go brute-forcer for directories, DNS subdomains, vhosts, and S3 buckets. Multiple modes (dir/dns/vhost/s3/fuzz). No recursion (use feroxbuster for that). CTF standard. gobuster dir -u URL -w wordlist, gobuster dns -d domain -w subdomains. Fast and reliable for single-depth enumeration and recon.
